On 27 September 2022, residents of bordering with Tajikistan villages gathered outside the regional state administration in Batken township demanding a meeting with a head of the State Committee on National Security to address their inquires regarding recent border protocol with Tajik officials.

(63 fatalities split among 9 events).

On 7 October 2022, female residents of Internatsionalnoye village in Leylek district of Batken region gathered near their homes in protest of a lack of security and financial support from the Kyrgyz government calling for the authorities to provide more aid and safety on the disputed border with Tajikistan following recent deadly armed clashes.

On 25 September 2018, 20-30 residents of the village of Maidan protested against the Chinese company operated project Shambesai in Kadamjai district after observing a group of Chinese nationals working at the mine. Protesters surrounded the Chinese workers and took them to the mine where they were questioned by the protesters. No violent interaction.

On 23 October 2022, residents of Batken region gathered near the town of Batken in support of incumbent president Sadyr Japarov calling for the country's public to reject opposition criticism of the Kyrgyz leadership and prevent anti-government disturbances in the nation.

On 22 October 2022, residents of Samarkandyk village in Batken region gathered near their homes to express their support for the country's leadership regarding resolution of the border disputes with Tajikistan and Uzbekistan calling for the Kyrgyz public to support president Sadyr Japarov and his aide in their efforts to settle demarcation and delimitation negotiations with the neighboring states.

On 10 November 2022, residents of the bordering villages with Tajikistan gathered near the town of Internatsionalnoye in Leylek district of Batken region calling for the Kyrgyz government to speed up renovation of damaged properties after military clashes between Kyrgyz and Tajik forces, provide access to drinking water in the villages, fix roads, provide power supply and subsidies to the families in need.

On 16 November 2022, residents of Chechme village in Kadamjay district of Batken region protested near their homes against transfer of control over the local water source to the Uzbek side in a border deal with Uzbekistan demanding that the Kyrgyz government and parliament revise the agreement and ensure water source remain under Kyrgyz control.

On 22 November 2022, dozens of Leylek district residents gathered outside the local administration in Kulundu village of Batken region in protest of alleged misuse and embezzlement of public funds and aid calling for the Kyrgyz authorities to probe the local councilmen for alleged misconduct while in office.

On 5 December 2022, women activists marched in the town of Kadamjai of Kadamjay district in Batken region against violence against women and domestic violence in the country calling for the government and public to do more to prevent and stop such occurrences in the nation.

On 3 December 2022, a verbal altercation between Kyrgyz and Tajik villagers by Somonien locality near the village of Kok-Tash in Batken region near the disputed border resulted in physical confrontation developing into throwing stones, after which a Kyrgyz villager discharged his hunting rifle firing 3 warning shots.

On 7 December 2022, students and social workers marched to raise awareness of domestic violence incidents in Suluktu township of Batken region, calling for local public to get involved in efforts to prevent violence against women in the area.

On 18 February 2023, female residents of Kara-Bak village in Batken region gathered outside the local administration in protest of delays with processing applications for social aid and child subsidies demanding that the authorities provide subsidies and aid.

On 17 April 2021, about 30 residents of the Tajik village of Khojai Alo threw stones at the workers of the Batken regional water service on the disputed border in the village of Ak-Sai in Batken region. It happened following a heated verbal exchange where Tajik villagers demanded that Kyrgyz side halt work at the Golovnoy water collector which is disputed by both countries. Border services of both states arrived on the scene to mitigate the conflict. Some residents of Khoja-i-Alo were reporting a shooting in the area, however local sources and administration denied there was a shooting. Press service of governor of Batken region also denied Tajik villagers used rocks against Kyrgyz water service employees at the water collector.

Border incidents between the two nations are common as they have yet to define about 50 per cent of their 970-km-long common border.
